excerpt: The Qur'an refers to the heart of the Messenger of Allah because the verses of the Qur'an were revealed to the heart of the Prophet.
source_location_hint: Discussion of Qur'anic nomenclature and the heart as recipient of revelation

excerpt: The Qur'an is what was revealed in Arabic. It will not be translated into any language; it will not be called the Qur'an. Arabic verses of the Qur'an have been made obligatory in prayer.
source_location_hint: Chapter on ritual purity and prayer requirements

excerpt: The first stage of the revelation of the Qur'an was the cave of Hira in Makkah and the last destination was Jabal Rahmat, the plain of Arafat.
source_location_hint: Discussion of the chronology and geography of Qur'anic revelation

excerpt: The pen (the first intellect) wrote down the entire meaning of the Qur'an on the preserved tablet in the night of Qadr in the city of Ramadan. Then Allah revealed these meanings slowly to the pure heart of the Messenger of Allah over a period of 23 years.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of the Preserved Tablet and the Night of Decree
